name: Baghrir (Thousand-Hole Pancake)
description: A delicious Moroccan pancake with thousands of tiny holes that perfectly capture honey and amlou!

instructions.text: In a bowl, mix the semolina, flour, sugar, salt, and active dry yeast.
Pour in the warm water and mix briefly to avoid lumps.
Blend the batter for 4 minutes to obtain a smooth and homogeneous mixture.
Add the baking powder. Then blend again for 1 minute to incorporate it well.
Let the batter rest for 1 hour at room temperature. It should be very liquid and form bubbles on the surface.
Cook the baghrir in a hot pan. Cook only on one side, until the surface is covered with holes and the edges are dry.
Set aside on a clean towel or paper towels to prevent them from getting damp and sticking to each other.
Repeat until all the batter is cooked.

tips: Using a crepe maker is easier for cooking and for a uniform baghrir shape.
You can store cooked baghrir in an airtight container in the refrigerator for 2 days. Warm them slightly before serving.
You can cook the pancakes in batches, store the batter in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours maximum, covering it with plastic wrap.
You can use extra-fine semolina for an even lighter, airier texture.
